landing.india.title: Designed for Indian Agriculture
landing.india.card1Body: Recommendations follow traditional and modern Indian farming seasons: Kharif, Rabi, and Zaid cycles for optimal planning.
landing.india.card2Body: Our AI is trained on authentic farming datasets from across India, ensuring locally relevant and accurate recommendations.
landing.india.card3Body: Customized advisory for different regions, considering local climate, soil types, and agricultural practices.
landing.india.note: In alignment with the vision of Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare, Government of India 🇮🇳
landing.cta.title: Start Planning Your Next Crop Cycle Today
landing.cta.subtitle: Simple • Reliable • Data-driven

landing.footer.note: This platform provides AI-based advisory suggestions. Always consult local agricultural experts for final decisions.

home.subtitle: Plan your next crop cycle with AI guidance built from weather, soil inputs, and field context.
home.trust.disclaimer: Advisory support only. SmartKrishi gives planning guidance, not guaranteed yield or market outcome.
home.snapshot.pendingCaption: Complete one crop assessment to unlock advisory history.
home.weather.title: Weather snapshot
home.weather.unavailable: Weather service is unavailable right now.
home.soil.title: Soil health summary
home.soil.missing: Start one crop assessment to build your soil summary card.
home.soil.summary.stable: Balanced pH and nutrients for a normal planning cycle.
home.soil.summary.watch: One or more soil indicators need correction before sowing.
home.soil.summary.strong: Strong nutrient profile for a high-confidence recommendation.
home.soil.phGood: pH is in the preferred band
home.alerts.title: Field alerts
home.alerts.noneBody: SmartKrishi will surface weather and pest alerts once live signals are available.
home.alerts.rainBody: Rainfall is elevated. Keep drainage ready before irrigation or fertilizer application.
home.alerts.humidityBody: Humidity is high. Scout leaves every 2-3 days for fungal patches and early pest signs.
home.alerts.clearBody: Conditions look stable. Follow planned irrigation and keep field scouting routine active.
home.tips.title: Tips of the day
home.tips.subtitle: Localized farming advice
home.tips.kharif1: Check bunds and drainage before the next rain spell to avoid standing water in kharif crops.
home.tips.kharif2: Split nitrogen application into smaller doses instead of one heavy top dressing.
home.tips.rabi1: Use early morning irrigation in rabi to reduce evaporation loss and improve soil moisture hold.
home.tips.rabi2: Watch for nutrient lock if soil pH drifts high after repeated irrigation.
home.tips.default1: Keep a short written log of rainfall, spray dates, and fertilizer timing for each field.
home.tips.default2: Scout one edge and one center patch of the field before any blanket spray decision.
home.tips.common: Advisory works best when soil values are updated before each sowing cycle.
home.features.title: Start from the task you need
home.features.description: Every feature card is built around a real farmer workflow, not a demo shortcut.
home.feature.crop.title: Crop planner

home.feature.guidance.meta: Unlock once you complete one crop assessment.
home.feature.disease.title: Leaf disease check

results.title: Field recommendation
results.subtitle: Ranked crop suggestions with actionable farm advisories.
results.error.title: Recommendation unavailable
results.error.body: The advisory service could not load your crop recommendation right now.
results.trustNote: Advisory support only. Yield can change based on seed quality, irrigation, local pests, and market conditions.
results.topChoicesDescription: Each option includes confidence, reason, and yield range to support practical field planning.
results.advisoryDescription: Open each section for irrigation, fertilizer, pest control, and weather watch actions.
results.reason.phManaged: Soil pH can be managed with input correction
results.sections.irrigation.title: Irrigation
results.sections.irrigation.summary: Water timing and field moisture actions
results.sections.fertilizer.title: Fertilizer
results.sections.fertilizer.summary: Nutrient correction and split application guidance
results.sections.pest.title: Pest control
results.sections.weather.title: Weather watch
results.advice.irrigation.heavy.1: Delay fresh irrigation until topsoil dries and drainage channels are clear.
results.advice.irrigation.heavy.2: Inspect low-lying patches for standing water before any fertilizer application.
results.advice.irrigation.heavy.3: Resume light irrigation only after root zone moisture starts dropping.
results.advice.irrigation.humid.1: Reduce irrigation frequency and inspect leaf wetness before watering again.
results.advice.irrigation.humid.2: Water in the morning so foliage dries faster during the day.
results.advice.irrigation.humid.3: Avoid excess standing moisture near the crop base.
results.advice.irrigation.normal.1: Use planned irrigation intervals and avoid flood watering where possible.
results.advice.irrigation.normal.2: Check soil moisture in one edge and one center patch before the next cycle.
results.advice.irrigation.normal.3: Keep irrigation timing consistent during early crop establishment.
results.advice.fertilizer.phLow: Apply lime or dolomite gradually to move acidic soil closer to the workable pH band.
results.advice.fertilizer.phHigh: Use organic matter or sulfur-based correction carefully to reduce alkalinity over time.
results.advice.fertilizer.cereal: Split nitrogen into 2-3 doses instead of one heavy application for cereal crops.
results.advice.fertilizer.cash: Balance nitrogen with potash support to maintain crop strength during branching and boll formation.
results.advice.fertilizer.general: Use a balanced NPK plan and avoid over-correcting any single nutrient.
results.advice.fertilizer.organic: Add compost or well-decomposed FYM to improve nutrient holding and soil structure.
results.advice.pest.humid.1: Scout leaves every 2-3 days for fungal spots, blight, and sucking pest activity.
results.advice.pest.humid.2: Remove visibly infected leaves early and keep tool hygiene strict.
results.advice.pest.humid.3: Use targeted spray only after field scouting confirms risk.
results.advice.pest.normal.1: Walk the field weekly and check both leaf surfaces for early pest signs.
results.advice.pest.normal.2: Remove heavily infested leaves and track affected patches instead of blanket spraying first.
results.advice.pest.normal.3: Keep weed growth controlled around the crop edge to reduce pest shelter.
results.advice.weather.heavy.1: Prepare drainage before the next rain pulse and keep fertilizer bags protected.
results.advice.weather.heavy.2: Avoid spraying just before expected rainfall to reduce chemical wash-off.
results.advice.weather.heavy.3: Inspect field bunds and entry points for runoff damage after rain.
results.advice.weather.normal.1: Conditions look stable. Follow planned irrigation and scouting intervals.
results.advice.weather.normal.2: Keep one weather check in the morning before any nutrient or spray decision.
results.advice.weather.normal.3: Update soil and weather inputs again if field conditions change sharply.
disease.title: Disease check
disease.subtitle: Check by leaf photo
disease.case.bacterial.t3: Ensure proper spacing for air circulation
new_results.loadingSteps: Evaluating crops • Checking season • Analyzing soil • Computing
new_results.noSuitableBody: No suitable crops found for your current inputs. Try adjusting soil parameters or selecting a different season.
new_results.advisoryHint: Open each section for irrigation, fertilizer, pest control, and weather guidance.
new_results.fallback.disclaimer: AI-based advisory. Please consult local agricultural experts for final decisions.
